title: Breadth-First Search, Queues, and Unweighted Shortest Paths · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for breadth-first search, queues, and unweighted shortest paths and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for deque-based shortest-path solver. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Maintain a FIFO frontier whose discovered distances advance in nondecreasing edge count.
Use deque append and popleft with explicit membership state and bounded duplicate work.
Assign distance and predecessor at first discovery and reconstruct a valid route after reaching the goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

For diagnose the hidden failure, solve one convenient seeded maze, omit revision, coordinate and wall invariants, topology oracle, frontier trace, changed algorithm or heuristic, callback ownership, keyboard and structured-text access, native Tk evidence, failure, recovery, and package transfer, then accept a screenshot as maze proof. This does not satisfy Bfs Changed Cases for deque-based shortest-path solver.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bfs Changed Cases
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Bfs Changed Cases: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 4153 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bfs Changed Cases.
steps.why: Retrieving Bfs Changed Cases through the concrete deque-based shortest-path solver case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bfs Changed Cases, keep Bfs Changed Cases connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bfs Changed Cases, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Bfs Changed Cases precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bfs Changed Cases, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A quiz puzzle team handles case 4153 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove an unweighted shortest route through FIFO layers and stable predecessors; all graph claims need an independent witness; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare reachable, unreachable, start-equals-goal, multiple-shortest-path, cyclic, and changed-neighbor-order cases.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Bfs Changed Cases, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One perfect maze is enough to test BFS.
